title: Active Knee Exoskeleton for Osteoarthritis Treatment
overview: Designed a wearable active exoskeleton to reduce Knee Adduction Moment (KAM) for mild-to-moderate knee osteoarthritis patients. The system features a bio-inspired polycentric mechanism that closely mimics natural knee kinematics.
problem: Knee osteoarthritis affects millions globally, causing pain and reduced mobility. Existing passive braces provide limited relief without addressing the root biomechanical cause — elevated Knee Adduction Moment during gait.
objectives: Reduce KAM during ambulation by applying corrective assistive torque
Mimic the knee's Instantaneous Center of Rotation using a 5-Bar Linkage
Achieve a ICR tracking error below 1 mm using Genetic Algorithm optimization
Provide real-time gait phase detection and assistive torque of 25 Nm
highlights: Polycentric 5-Bar Linkage optimized via Genetic Algorithm achieving 0.47 mm ICR tracking error
Remote cable-driven actuation system for reduced distal mass
FSM-based gait detection for adaptive assistive torque delivery
Impedance control strategy targeting 25 Nm assistive torque

title: Below-Knee Prosthetic Leg Fatigue Load Testing Mechanism
overview: Designed a fully mechanical walking mechanism that accurately simulates the human gait cycle — including heel-strike, stance, toe-off, and swing phases — for fatigue load testing of below-knee prosthetic legs.
problem: Prosthetic leg components require rigorous fatigue testing under realistic cyclic loading conditions. Existing test rigs either over-simplify the loading profile or are prohibitively expensive for small-scale R&D.
objectives: Simulate all four phases of the human gait cycle mechanically
Withstand vertical loads up to 100 kg
Keep cost low using DFMA principles and 3D printing
Select optimal mechanism using weighted decision matrices
highlights: Guided frame crank-link mechanism selected via weighted decision matrices
Full gait cycle simulation: heel-strike → stance → toe-off → swing
100 kg vertical load capacity with structural safety factors validated
PLA 3D printed prototype for validation before final fabrication

title: Autonomous Robot — Path Following & Obstacle Avoidance
overview: Designed and built a fully autonomous robot capable of navigating complex environments including line-following, wall-following, and 4-way junction obstacle avoidance with mobile app control.
problem: Autonomous navigation in unstructured environments requires robust sensor fusion and decision-making algorithms that can handle edge cases like dead zones and multi-path junctions.
objectives: Implement reliable line following using IR sensors
Add wall following mode using ultrasonic sensors
Handle 4-way junction decisions with obstacle avoidance
Provide mobile app manual override and dead-zone recovery
highlights: Multi-mode navigation: line following, wall following, obstacle avoidance
Intelligent 4-way junction handling with priority logic
Mobile app control with real-time mode switching
Dead-zone recovery algorithm for stuck-state resolution

title: IoT-Based Temperature & Humidity Monitoring System
overview: Developed an IoT-based monitoring system for industrial environments to track temperature and humidity in real-time with cloud logging and alert systems.
problem: At Future Fibres Lanka, composite manufacturing requires strict environmental monitoring. Manual checks were time-consuming and missed critical fluctuations affecting product quality.
objectives: Monitor temperature and humidity continuously across multiple zones
Log data in real-time to cloud via ThingSpeak
Generate alerts when parameters exceed set thresholds
Provide web dashboard for remote monitoring
highlights: Dual sensor integration: DHT22 (temp/humidity) + DS18B20 (precision temperature)
Real-time cloud data logging at configurable intervals
ThingSpeak dashboard with visual analytics
Deployed in industrial composite manufacturing environment

title: Smart Medibox — IoT Embedded Project
overview: Designed an intelligent medication storage system with environmental monitoring and automated control to ensure medication efficacy.
problem: Many medications require specific temperature and light conditions. Non-compliance leads to reduced efficacy or safety risks.
objectives: Monitor temperature, humidity, and light levels in real-time
Automate environmental control (cooling, shading) based on sensor data
Provide MQTT-based remote dashboard via Node-RED
Simulate and validate on Wokwi before hardware deployment
highlights: MQTT protocol for lightweight real-time data transmission
Node-RED dashboard with live sensor visualization
Automated servo-based light control system
Full Wokwi simulation before physical prototyping

title: Gearbox Design — Combat Robot Weapons
overview: Designed a compact, high-torque multi-speed gearbox for combat robot weapon systems, supporting both vertical and horizontal spinner configurations.
problem: Combat robot spinners require rapid spin-up and sustained high rotational energy. Off-the-shelf gearboxes don't meet the compact size, torque density, and impact resistance requirements.
objectives: Design for both vertical and horizontal spinner weapon types
Maximize torque-to-weight ratio
Perform full gear selection, shaft design, and power transmission calculations
Ensure impact-resistant housing
highlights: Custom gear ratio optimization for rapid weapon spin-up
Full shaft design with bearing selection and life calculations
Impact-resistant housing design with safety factors
Dual-configuration support for vertical and horizontal spinners

title: Reverse Engineering — Bicycle Disc Brake System
overview: Performed comprehensive reverse engineering of a bicycle disc brake system including material characterization, 3D modeling, FEA simulation, and generative design optimization.
problem: Understanding the design intent and material selection of existing brake systems is essential for iterative improvement. Reverse engineering provides insights not available in commercial documentation.
objectives: Identify materials via burn tests, density measurements, and Rockwell hardness tests
Create accurate 3D models from physical measurements
Perform FEA to validate structural performance
Apply generative design for weight reduction
highlights: Multi-method material identification: burn tests, density, Rockwell hardness
High-accuracy 3D reverse-engineered models in Fusion 360
FEA stress and thermal analysis under braking loads
Generative design achieving 23% weight reduction

title: Autonomous Car Wash Robot
overview: Designed and built an autonomous car wash robot as part of a mechatronics group project, integrating mechanical, electrical, and software systems.
problem: Manual car washing is time-consuming and inconsistent. An automated system can deliver repeatable wash quality with minimal human intervention.
objectives: Design mechanical carriage and spray system in SolidWorks
Integrate Arduino Mega with motor drivers and sensors
Enable WiFi control via ESP module and MIT App Inventor app
Implement automatic wash cycle sequences
highlights: Full mechanical design with linear carriage and brush systems
WiFi-enabled mobile app for remote control and cycle selection
Automated wash sequence: pre-rinse, soap, scrub, rinse, dry
Multi-axis actuator coordination via Arduino Mega

title: Optimization of Production Line — Aruna Tea Factory
overview: Conducted a comprehensive production line optimization study at Aruna Tea Factory, using simulation-based analysis to identify and resolve operational bottlenecks.
problem: Inefficiencies in the withering and packing stages were limiting throughput and increasing production cycle time.
objectives: Map current production flow and identify bottlenecks
Simulate production scenarios with proposed changes
Propose scheduling optimizations and equipment modifications
Quantify potential throughput improvements
highlights: Identified withering (primary) and packing (secondary) as key bottleneck stages
Simulation-based scenario testing for multiple configuration options
Proposed scheduling changes reducing cycle time by estimated 18%
Equipment modification recommendations for packing section

title: STEM Development & Experiment Kit — Feasibility Study
shortDesc: Comprehensive feasibility study (market, technical, financial) for an eco-friendly STEM experiment kit targeting children.
overview: Conducted a comprehensive three-pillar feasibility study for an eco-friendly STEM kit product targeting primary school children in Sri Lanka.
problem: STEM education gaps in regional Sri Lanka schools are partly due to lack of affordable, hands-on experimental tools that engage students practically.
objectives: Assess market demand and competitive landscape
Evaluate technical production feasibility
Model financial projections and break-even analysis
Identify and mitigate key project risks
highlights: Market analysis covering regional school demand and competitor pricing
Technical feasibility assessment for eco-friendly material sourcing
5-year financial model with NPV, IRR, and break-even analysis
Risk matrix with mitigation strategies for 12 identified risks

title: Industrial Engineering - Based Productivity & Layout Optimization: A Case Study
shortDesc: Industrial Engineering case study on productivity and layout optimization at Abans Auto (Pvt) Ltd.
overview: Optimized assembly and inspection processes at the Hero Motorcycle Assembly Plant using facility layout improvements, material handling reduction, and safety-focused test track design.
problem: Suboptimal layout, bidirectional movement paths, shared-road testing, and reactive WIP management limited throughput and raised safety risks.
objectives: Optimize assembly and inspection flow for higher plant productivity
Reduce material handling waste and eliminate dead-end workstations
Establish a dedicated drive-through test track and safer workflow
Implement strategic safety stock to reduce WIP delays
highlights: Identified PDI/QC as primary constraints with 75 minutes/unit
Reduced material search waste by 90 minutes per batch
Proposed a drive-through workstation layout and standalone test track
Developed a data-driven inventory strategy for stable throughput
